channel.title: Conspiracy Theory on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Conspiracy Theory on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Nibiru cataclysm
item.description:
The Nibiru cataclysm is a predicted disastrous encounter between Earth and a large planetary object, often referred to as Nibiru or Planet X. This doomsday scenario was first proposed in 1995 by Nancy Lieder, who claimed to receive messages from extraterrestrials. The idea has since spread through various internet groups and has been linked to the 2012 phenomenon and other astronomical events. Astronomers and planetary scientists have widely rejected the Nibiru cataclysm as pseudoscience, stating there is no scientific evidence to support such claims.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nibiru_cataclysm

item.title: Milton William Cooper
item.description:
Milton William “Bill” Cooper (1943–2001) was an American author and radio broadcaster recognized as an influential figure within militia and conspiracy theory circles. In his 1991 book Behold a Pale Horse, Cooper promoted elaborate theories involving the Illuminati, government collusion with extraterrestrials, and the Kennedy assassination. On November 5, 2001, Cooper was fatally shot by Apache County sheriff’s deputies during an armed confrontation at his home in Eagar, Arizona, following years of evading a federal arrest warrant for tax evasion.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milton_William_Cooper

item.title: Linda Moulton Howe
item.description:
Linda Moulton Howe is an American investigative journalist and documentary filmmaker who rose to prominence for her reporting on environmental issues and, later, for her work in the field of ufology. She gained significant attention in 1980 for producing the documentary A Strange Harvest, which explored cattle mutilation theories and alleged extraterrestrial involvement in government activities. Throughout her career, Howe has contributed to numerous television programs and media projects related to UFOs, crop circles, and alien abductions, while frequently appearing as a guest on platforms like Coast to Coast AM. While some supporters regard her as a leading figure in the investigation of unexplained phenomena, critics such as Joe Nickell have characterized her work as credulous and lacking in verified evidence.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linda_Moulton_Howe

item.title: The Flying Saucers Are Real
item.description:
Donald Keyhoe’s 1950 book, The Flying Saucers Are Real, investigated United States Air Force reports of UFOs between 1947 and 1950. The book, which followed a similar article published in True magazine, popularized many ideas in ufology. Keyhoe argued that the Air Force had a policy of concealing UFO existence until 1949, after which it shifted to gradual revelation. He also contended that Earth had been visited by extraterrestrials for two centuries, with increased activity after the 1945 atomic bomb test.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Flying_Saucers_Are_Real

item.title: William L. Moore and His Contributions to UFOlogy
item.description:
William L. Moore, born in 1943, is an author and former UFO researcher known for his work from the late 1970s to the late 1980s. He co-authored books with Charles Berlitz, including “The Philadelphia Experiment - Project Invisibility” and “The Roswell Incident,” which alleged extraterrestrial involvement in the Roswell incident. Moore also circulated the Majestic 12 documents, which purported to reveal a secret government group overseeing UFOs. Critics have described his books as unsubstantiated and his work on the Majestic 12 documents as fraudulent.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bill_Moore_%28ufologist%29

item.title: Ratanabá conspiracy theory
item.description:
Ratanabá is a conspiracy theory about a lost city in the Brazilian Amazon rainforest, allegedly built by extraterrestrial beings. The theory was initiated by Urandir Fernandes de Oliveira and spread through social media, becoming a viral meme. Despite being debunked by archaeologists and dismissed as pseudoscientific, the theory gained traction, even being promoted by former Culture Secretary Mário Frias. The spread of misinformation related to Ratanabá has also threatened historical heritage sites, leading to official repudiation.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ratanab%C3%A1

item.title: Above Majestic
item.description:
Above Majestic is a 2018 documentary film that proposes a global conspiracy theory involving reptilian aliens who secretly control world governments. The film posits that these aliens inhabit Antarctica, are preparing for an invasion, and are behind the September 11 attacks as a means to hide their conspiracy. According to Vice, the documentary claims these aliens control governments and banks. Researchers reported in 2021 that a QAnon-linked Twitter user may have aided in the film’s production.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Above_Majestic
